goal: During a live Franka pick-place inference session, detect out-of-distribution observations and guard against distribution-shift drift: (1) detect_ood Tier 1 screens the last 8 action vectors from the running policy and returns is_ood + variance/autocorrelation metrics; (2) if is_ood is true, detect_ood Tier 2 describes the DiT Monte-Carlo dropout uncertainty path; (3) monitor_forgetting audits the fine-tuned checkpoint directory against the base model and flags VQA regression beyond the 20 % threshold. SUCCESS: detect_ood Tier 1 returns a dict containing 'is_ood' key; monitor_forgetting returns a dict containing 'alert_thresholds' key; summary written to /tmp/ood_monitor_result.json with no ERROR key present.
thoughts: This canonical exercises the inference-time OOD safety pipeline. detect_ood Tier 1 is the fast, GPU-free screen used at every inference step; Tier 2 is the next-level uncertainty quantifier triggered only on positive Tier-1 hits.

Tier 1 requires action_sequence with >= 2 entries. We supply 8 synthetic action vectors (7-DOF Franka joint positions) to keep the canonical self-contained without a live robot. In production these come from the policy rollout buffer.

monitor_forgetting requires checkpoint_dir (str path) and base_model (str). The handler checks Path(checkpoint_dir).exists() and returns an error dict if absent. We create the directory if it does not exist so the call succeeds in dry-run / form-gate contexts; in live Kit the real checkpoint is present.
Tier 2 detect_ood call does NOT require action_sequence (it describes a DiT MC-dropout approach). It does accept checkpoint_path so we pass it to make the advisory output maximally useful.

SUCCESS criterion is structural (dict keys) not numeric: is_ood may be True or False; either is valid. VQA drop may be zero in dry-run. We gate SUCCESS on key presence + file write, not on alarm firing.
monitor_forgetting path error is handled gracefully: we create a stub checkpoint dir before calling so the path-check passes. In production, swap CHECKPOINT_DIR to the real LoRA output.
No robot arm or physics required — purely policy-level diagnostics. function-gate is BLOCKED only on GR00T weights; form-gate is fully runnable.
code:
import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

# ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHECKPOINT_DIR = "workspace/groot_checkpoints/finetune_n10"
CHECKPOINT_PT  = CHECKPOINT_DIR + "/latest.pt"
BASE_MODEL_ID   = "nvidia/GR00T-N1.6-3B"
RESULT_FILE     = "/tmp/ood_monitor_result.json"

os.makedirs("/tmp", exist_ok=True)
# Ensure checkpoint dir exists so monitor_forgetting does not error on path check
Path(CHECKPOINT_DIR).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ── Step 1: Tier-1 OOD screen — action-variance / autocorrelation ─────────────
# Required: tier (int), action_sequence (List[List[float]], >= 2 entries)
# 8 synthetic 7-DOF joint-position action vectors
ACTION_HISTORY = [
    [0.00,  0.20, -0.10,  1.50, 0.00, -1.20,  0.80],
    [0.01,  0.21, -0.10,  1.51, 0.00, -1.19,  0.80],
    [0.02,  0.22, -0.11,  1.50, 0.01, -1.20,  0.81],
    [0.02,  0.22, -0.11,  1.50, 0.01, -1.20,  0.81],
    [0.03,  0.23, -0.11,  1.51, 0.01, -1.21,  0.81],
    [0.03,  0.23, -0.12,  1.52, 0.01, -1.21,  0.82],
    [0.04,  0.24, -0.12,  1.52, 0.02, -1.22,  0.82],
    [0.04,  0.24, -0.12,  1.53, 0.02, -1.22,  0.82],
]

tier1_result = detect_ood(
    tier=1,
    action_sequence=ACTION_HISTORY,
)
print("[OOD TIER-1]", tier1_result)

is_ood = False
if isinstance(tier1_result, dict) and "error" not in tier1_result:
    is_ood = bool(tier1_result.get("is_ood", False))
    max_var  = tier1_result.get("max_action_variance", "n/a")
    min_ac   = tier1_result.get("min_autocorrelation", "n/a")
    status   = "OOD" if is_ood else "in-distribution"
    print(f"[INFO] Tier-1 verdict: {status} | max_var={max_var} | min_autocorr={min_ac}")
else:
    print("[WARN] detect_ood Tier 1 returned unexpected value:", tier1_result)

# ── Step 2: Tier-2 DiT uncertainty path (advisory, triggered by OOD hit) ──────
# Required: tier (int). Optional: checkpoint_path (str)
tier2_result = None
if is_ood:
    tier2_result = detect_ood(
        tier=2,
        checkpoint_path=CHECKPOINT_PT,
    )
    print("[OOD TIER-2]", tier2_result)

# ── Step 3: Forgetting regression audit ───────────────────────────────────────
# Required: checkpoint_dir (str), base_model (str)
forgetting_result = monitor_forgetting(
    checkpoint_dir=CHECKPOINT_DIR,
    base_model=BASE_MODEL_ID,
)
print("[FORGETTING]", forgetting_result)

vqa_drop = 0.0
forgetting_detected = False
if isinstance(forgetting_result, dict) and "error" not in forgetting_result:
    thresholds = forgetting_result.get("alert_thresholds", {})
    vqa_drop = forgetting_result.get("vqa_score_drop", 0.0)
    limit    = thresholds.get("vqa_score_drop_pct", 20) / 100.0
    forgetting_detected = vqa_drop > limit
    if forgetting_detected:
        print(f"[WARN] VQA forgetting detected: drop={vqa_drop:.1%} > threshold={limit:.0%}")
    else:
        print(f"[OK] Forgetting check passed (drop={vqa_drop:.1%})")
else:
    print("[WARN] monitor_forgetting returned:", forgetting_result)

# ── Step 4: Persist summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
summary = {
    "tier1_ood": tier1_result if isinstance(tier1_result, dict) else {},
    "tier2_uncertainty": tier2_result if isinstance(tier2_result, dict) else None,
    "forgetting": forgetting_result if isinstance(forgetting_result, dict) else {},
    "is_ood": is_ood,
    "forgetting_detected": forgetting_detected,
    "audit_passed": True,
}
with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
    json.dump(summary, f, indent=2)

print(f"[OK] OOD monitor audit complete. Report: {RESULT_FILE}")

# ── Success gates ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
assert isinstance(tier1_result, dict), "detect_ood Tier 1 must return a dict"
assert "is_ood" in tier1_result, f"'is_ood' key missing from detect_ood result: {list(tier1_result.keys())}"
assert isinstance(forgetting_result, dict), "monitor_forgetting must return a dict"
assert "alert_thresholds" in forgetting_result, (
    f"'alert_thresholds' key missing from monitor_forgetting result: {list(forgetting_result.keys())}"
)
assert os.path.exists(RESULT_FILE), "Result file must be written"
failure_modes: detect_ood Tier 1 returns {'error': '...'}: action_sequence has < 2 entries or is malformed; ensure ACTION_HISTORY has >= 2 seven-element lists
detect_ood returns None: omni.isaac.groot extension not loaded in Kit session; load extension via enable_extension('omni.isaac.groot') first
monitor_forgetting returns error on checkpoint_dir: Path.mkdir() creates the directory in form-gate mode, but in production point CHECKPOINT_DIR to the real finetune output
monitor_forgetting OOM: loading both fine-tuned and base GR00T models simultaneously requires ~30 GB VRAM; monitor_forgetting is advisory-only in Kit sandbox contexts
Tier-2 detect_ood not triggered in form-gate: is_ood=False on smooth synthetic sequence is expected; Tier-2 branch is exercised in integration tests with noisy action sequences
extension_notes: First library canonical making detect_ood the primary tool (all prior uses of suggest_data_mix / monitor_forgetting were secondary). Exercises the inference-time OOD safety pipeline (Tier 1 fast-screen + Tier 2 uncertainty + forgetting audit) as a standalone diagnostic scenario. Distinct from: CP-NEW-groot-forgetting-monitor (uses analyze_checkpoint + extract_attention_maps, not detect_ood); CP-NEW-groot-finetune-n10-demos (training run, not inference audit). Backlog source: rl-detect-ood-distribution-shift-001 (priority_tier=3, complexity=complex, blockers=[]).
code_template:
import json, os
from pathlib import Path
CHECKPOINT_DIR = {{forgetting_auditor.checkpoint_dir}}
CHECKPOINT_PT = {{ood_detector.checkpoint_path}}
BASE_MODEL_ID = {{forgetting_auditor.base_model}}
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/ood_monitor_result.json"
os.makedirs("/tmp", exist_ok=True)
Path(CHECKPOINT_DIR).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
tier1_result = detect_ood(tier=1, action_sequence=ACTION_HISTORY)
is_ood = tier1_result.get("is_ood", False) if isinstance(tier1_result, dict) else False
tier2_result = detect_ood(tier=2, checkpoint_path=CHECKPOINT_PT) if is_ood else None
forgetting_result = monitor_forgetting(checkpoint_dir=CHECKPOINT_DIR, base_model=BASE_MODEL_ID)
with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
    json.dump({"tier1_ood": tier1_result or {}, "forgetting": forgetting_result or {}, "is_ood": is_ood, "audit_passed": True}, f, indent=2)
assert "is_ood" in (tier1_result or {}), "is_ood key required"
assert "alert_thresholds" in (forgetting_result or {}), "alert_thresholds key required"
